skill.name: bug-diagnose-and-root-cause-and-fix
skill.description: 【Bug 诊断专用】当遇到程序错误、异常行为、性能问题、功能故障时必须使用本技能。触发关键词：报错、Bug、问题、异常、故障、不工作、诊断、排查、修复。本技能提供系统性根因分析，输出详细诊断报告和可执行修复方案，包含代码位置引用、步骤指导、注意事项。仅提供分析和方案，不直接修改代码。
skill.summary: Diagnose software bugs with evidence-based root cause analysis and structured repair guidance.

security_audit.summary: Static findings for Ruby-style backtick execution are false positives caused by Markdown inline code examples at SKILL.md:60, SKILL.md:69, SKILL.md:76, and SKILL.md:97. The weak cryptography and high-entropy findings are also unsupported by the visible Markdown content, but the skill does intentionally instruct Bash-based diagnostic commands at SKILL.md:25.

medium_findings.title: External Diagnostic Command Guidance
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs the assistant to use Bash for diagnostic commands such as git log, git blame, and npm list. This is legitimate for bug investigation, but it can execute local commands in a user workspace and should remain limited to read-only diagnostics.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The instruction explicitly mentions Bash and concrete diagnostic commands. The examples are normal development commands, so the risk is operational rather than malicious.

low_findings.title: Markdown Inline Code Misclassified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged Ruby or shell backtick execution, but the cited lines contain Markdown inline placeholders for file paths, line numbers, and commands in an output template. No executable Ruby or shell code is present at those locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged text is visible Markdown formatting, not a runtime language construct. The surrounding content describes report formatting rather than executable code.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Finding Not Supported
low_findings.description: The static analyzer reported a weak cryptographic algorithm at the description line. The line describes bug diagnosis in Chinese and contains no visible cryptographic algorithm, key handling, hashing, or encryption behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited line has no cryptographic API or algorithm name. This appears to be a pattern-matching false positive against natural language text.

low_findings.title: Entropy Heuristic Not Supported by File Content
low_findings.description: The static analyzer reported high file entropy at the start of the Markdown file. The reviewed file is readable text with front matter and Chinese documentation, and no binary, encrypted, or encoded payload was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file content is plainly readable Markdown throughout the reviewed 98 lines. The entropy score is plausibly affected by non-Latin text, not obfuscation.

dangerous_patterns.title: Bash Command Execution Requested for Diagnostics
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill asks the assistant to run Bash diagnostic commands during code review. This pattern should be restricted to necessary read-only commands and should avoid commands that alter files, install packages, or transmit data.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command guidance is explicit, but the named examples are common local diagnostics. No evidence of malicious command content or exfiltration was found.

content.value_statement: Software bugs often need structured investigation before code changes are safe. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through evidence-based root cause analysis and repair planning.
actual_capabilities: Collects symptoms, reproduction steps, error messages, and environment details.
Guides review of relevant code files, functions, and recent changes.
Uses diagnostic commands such as git history and dependency inspection when appropriate.
Builds evidence-based hypotheses and verifies them against code or logs.
Produces a structured diagnosis report with file and line references.
Recommends repair steps and validation checks without directly modifying code.
limitations: It provides analysis and guidance, not automatic code edits.
It depends on accurate logs, reproduction steps, and repository access.
Command use should be limited to safe diagnostic actions.
It may need human judgment for ambiguous production incidents.

use_cases.title: Investigate Application Errors
use_cases.description: Trace exceptions, failing features, or unexpected behavior to likely code causes.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Fix Plan
use_cases.description: Turn an observed defect into ordered repair steps and validation checks.

use_cases.title: Triage Regression Reports
use_cases.description: Compare symptoms with recent code changes and identify likely regression points.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Bug Diagnosis
prompt_templates.scenario: A user has an error message and wants a clear first analysis.
prompt_templates.prompt: Diagnose this bug. Ask for missing details if needed, then identify likely causes and next checks: [paste symptoms, error message, and steps].

prompt_templates.title: Root Cause Report
prompt_templates.scenario: A developer needs a structured report for a known failing workflow.
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this failure and produce a root cause report with evidence, affected files, repair options, and validation steps: [describe workflow and logs].

prompt_templates.title: Regression Investigation
prompt_templates.scenario: A team suspects a recent change introduced the bug.
prompt_templates.prompt: Investigate this suspected regression. Review relevant code and recent changes, then explain the most likely change that caused it: [describe bug and timeframe].

prompt_templates.title: Advanced Fix Strategy
prompt_templates.scenario: A senior engineer wants tradeoffs before implementing a fix.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a fix strategy for this bug. Compare at least two approaches, list risks, name verification commands, and recommend the safest path: [provide context].

output_examples.input: A login request returns a server error after a dependency update.
output_examples.output: The skill returns a diagnosis report that links the failure to changed authentication behavior, identifies affected files, and gives repair and test steps.

output_examples.input: A background job is slow after a recent release.
output_examples.output: The skill outlines performance hypotheses, points to recent code changes, recommends measurements, and proposes a fix plan with validation checks.

output_examples.input: A UI action works locally but fails in production.
output_examples.output: The skill compares environment differences, reviews relevant code paths, and recommends targeted checks before proposing a production-safe fix.

best_practices: Provide exact error messages, reproduction steps, and recent change history.
Allow only diagnostic commands that are necessary for the investigation.
Verify each conclusion against code, logs, or observed behavior.
anti_patterns: Do not ask the skill to edit files directly.
Do not run destructive commands during diagnosis.
Do not accept a root cause that lacks evidence.

faq.question: Does this skill modify code?
faq.answer: No. It produces analysis, repair guidance, and validation steps.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: It is listed for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: What information should I provide?
faq.answer: Provide symptoms, error messages, reproduction steps, relevant files, and recent changes.

faq.question: Can it run commands?
faq.answer: It may guide safe diagnostic commands, such as git history or dependency checks.

faq.question: Is it useful for performance bugs?
faq.answer: Yes. It can structure hypotheses, measurements, and validation steps for performance issues.

faq.question: What should I review before applying a fix?
faq.answer: Review the evidence, affected files, side effects, and validation plan.
